summary: The PoC successfully verified that multiple nodes use Object.assign(JSON.parse(userInput)) without filtering dangerous keys, confirming the prototype pollution vulnerability pattern exists in production code.
expected_security_impact: The verification should confirm that Object.assign + JSON.parse patterns exist in multiple nodes without __proto__ filtering, demonstrating the prototype pollution attack vector.
actual_observed_result: Verification confirmed vulnerable Object.assign + JSON.parse patterns in Zulip, Zendesk, Paddle, and CustomerIo nodes without proper key filtering for __proto__, constructor, and prototype keys.
verification_method: Static code analysis searching for Object.assign(JSON.parse()) patterns and checking for presence of __proto__ key filtering. Runtime demonstration of prototype pollution in JavaScript.
evidence_produced_by_run: Zulip.node.ts contains Object.assign(body, JSON.parse(additionalFieldsJson)) without key filtering
Zendesk.node.ts contains Object.assign(body, JSON.parse(additionalFieldsJson)) without key filtering
Paddle.node.ts contains Object.assign + JSON.parse patterns at multiple locations without key filtering
CustomerIo.node.ts contains Object.assign + JSON.parse patterns at multiple locations without key filtering
Runtime test demonstrated that Object.assign with __proto__ in parsed JSON pollutes Object.prototype
Vulnerability confirmed: Attack vector exists for prototype pollution

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
reproduction.pinned_commit: HEAD of n8n repository at time of audit
